meta.setting: Plain churchyards, grave fences, bell frames, borrowed shelter, and road-edge charity around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with refuge, mourning, public memory, charity, rumor, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Old northern California town buildings, courthouse memory, and civic/religious town texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional mining communities, families, roads, and settlement pressure.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Native displacement context, handled carefully without conversion spectacle.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Mission-Church Yards region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Bell Rope, Grave Fence, and Borrowed Shelter
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: object, weather or track, plain meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing the yard as refuge, public memory, mourning place, charity point, and scene engine
closing blockquote that gives a generated yard sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate a churchyard page for funerals, shelter, charity, rumor, cultural tension, and uneasy refuge.
Keep religious details generic and period-grounded; do not invent Native conversion drama as spectacle.
Use graves, bells, fences, borrowed blankets, meal baskets, weather, and the names people will not speak as practical scene tools.
The yard should feel peaceful only until somebody reads the newest board.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: A churchyard is where public mercy and public memory argue under the same bell.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A churchyard scene should ask who gets shelter, who is mourned publicly, who is buried quietly, and who hears confession without being asked.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western churchyard and shelter language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/mission-church-yards.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not invent Native conversion drama, sacred rites, or fake religious language
do not make the churchyard clean, harmless, or sentimental by default
do not use modern signage, vehicles, electric lights, plastic props, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about refuge, mourning, charity, public memory, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
